sections.content: Accidental DSC distress alerts happen more often than expected. If you accidentally trigger a distress alert, you must cancel it immediately to prevent unnecessary deployment of SAR resources. Failure to cancel a false alert is a serious violation of maritime radio regulations.

sections.title: Immediate Cancellation Required
sections.content: If you accidentally send a DSC distress alert, you MUST cancel it immediately by broadcasting on Channel 16: "ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS, ALL STATIONS — THIS IS [vessel name and MMSI] — CANCEL MY DISTRESS ALERT OF [date/time UTC] — MASTER [name]."

After making the voice cancellation on Channel 16, also cancel the DSC alert using your radio's DSC menu if it supports this function. Coast stations may contact you to confirm the cancellation. Always cooperate fully with authorities.

sections.prompt: What should you do after accidentally sending a false DSC distress alert?

options.text: Turn off the radio and hope no one noticed
Wait for a coast station to call you first
Cancel it immediately on Channel 16 with a voice broadcast
Send another distress alert to override the first

sections.explanation: You must immediately broadcast a cancellation on Channel 16, identifying your vessel and the time of the false alert. This prevents unnecessary SAR operations.
